item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is a really interesting SCP but I got one question.

When it explained that if a person in it's the blast radius they would vanish and will be replaced a ghost version of them or are semi see-through?

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: The images used in this article are cc-compliant with the SCP wiki’s license (CC BY-SA 3.0), as both are present on Wikimedia Commons here and here in order.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This has received a minor rewrite, clarifying some points raised above, slightly modifying tone in other places, and adding one or two minor pieces of fluff.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: give a man a smartphone and shoot him with a ghost bullet and boom: you have the world's best source of intelligence. infiltrate any facility, exfiltrate intel from anywhere.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It was a pretty good SCP, and I was probably going to upvote it. Then came counter-terrorists win, which turned that probably into a definitely.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: These aren't much like any other ghost skips.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I dunno. I think my disconnect is that these are bombs what turn people into ghosts, but in an anomalous world where ghosts can exist, you really don't need special bombs to turn into a ghost. You know what I mean? It's a well-written article, but in-universe, doesn't seem like a terribly special effect.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A good example of how you don't need a Thing What Kills You for horror. This was truly disturbing.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Upvoted. What really stood out to me is how wonderfully clear the writing was. Everyone can take lessons in clarity from this article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I know that MTF names are supposed to be a little less serious, but "Counter-Terrorists Win" feels a bit too silly. Anyone else agree?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Rounds definitely convey the full force of the payload in my head canon. (Even grazing.) This skip violates laws of physics.

RE: detection, SCP-2283-A definitely had a unique chemical signature and thus you can train dogs, or calibrate sensors, to detect it. I also imagine the ordnance itself in the form of SCP-XXXX assembled looks … unusual. Perhaps Foundation EOD personnel know what to look for. The resemblance to regular ordnance is very close.

Concealed bombs etc. obviously are undetectable. I'd assume the Foundation intelligence units have caught on to specific threats of more than *just a bomb.* is a terrorist using anomalous ordnance just gonna make the standard threat? Maybe. But likely not.

If these factors need clarification, will add in the minor update I'm planning that will be final revision.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: maybe through chemical analysis? Like you are gonna have to disable all IEDs anyways, and it's stated that the explosive compounds of this SCP is similar to but not the same as RDX.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Maybe I missed this, but if SCP-2283 look like regular bombs, how do the field agents know which bombs to contain? If you can only tell the difference between regular bombs and anomalous bombs after they've exploded… well, that's a problem.

Also, I like the idea of the rifle rounds, but I have to wonder if a normally non-fatal wound (ex. hand or foot) would still ghostify you. I don't know how explode-y incendiary rounds are, so maybe this is an academic question.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you! I'm interested in expanding this concept. If the Foundation is a worldwide scientific entity, it surely has its own academic publishing organizations.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I may fix this in an update.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I am surprised that the academic exposition felt meaningful here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
While the "Imagine" line was a little melodramatic1, this is a great idea otherwise executed wonderfully. +1

Footnotes
1. I didn't need to be directly told to imagine that, you already did a good job putting me there with the rest of the piece.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
GENKI DAMA

Obligatory joke aside, this is great. I posted the majority of my feedback in the draft thread, so I'm just gonna say that bombs what make you ghosts is still a great concept and will not stop being great.
